jokes.text: It's Not the Size of the Boat: Embracing Life with a Micro-Penis
jokes.setup: Formal library debt collection meeting about an overdue book
jokes.punchline: The book title is hilariously embarrassing and completely inappropriate for Ron's character
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subverts expectations about Ron's hypermasculine character while setting up Tammy's manipulation. The formal delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tammy. My ex-wife Tammy likes to check in every so often and make sure I'm doing okay. And if I am, she tries to [bleep] everything up.
jokes.setup: Ron describing what seems like caring ex-wife behavior
jokes.punchline: The reveal that she only cares so she can sabotage him
jokes.explanation: Great character setup that efficiently establishes the toxic relationship dynamic while being genuinely funny.

jokes.text: If she binds your hands and you can't reach your phone, just try to chew yourself free.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving Ron safety advice about meeting his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: The advice escalates to absurd kidnapping scenario precautions
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation joke that plays into Leslie's overprotective nature and Tammy's dangerous reputation.

jokes.text: Whale tale. Whale tale. She's flashing a whale tale. Abort. Abort.
jokes.setup: Tom acting as lookout for Ron to avoid Tammy
jokes.punchline: Military-style code for something as mundane as visible underwear
jokes.explanation: Tom's characteristic overcomplication of simple observations with military terminology.

jokes.text: I was just checking myself for scoliosis. - And? - Straight as an arrow. Just like somebody else I know.
jokes.setup: Tammy explaining her provocative position as medical self-examination
jokes.punchline: The double entendre about Ron being 'straight' (both posture and sexuality)
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo but relies on fairly obvious double meaning. Character-appropriate for Tammy's manipulative seduction.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, she's amazing.
jokes.setup: Tammy being sexually manipulative in front of everyone
jokes.punchline: Tom's inappropriate admiration despite witnessing manipulation
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that shows Tom's poor judgment and sets up his later involvement.

jokes.text: A lovely, intelligent, self-possessed pediatric surgeon named Wendy. - Sounds like a real whore.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his new girlfriend in glowing, respectful terms
jokes.punchline: Tammy's immediate, crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Ron's respectful description and Tammy's jealous, vulgar response. Great character work.

jokes.text: Good day, Leslie. - Good... good day.
jokes.setup: Ron's formal exit after confronting Tammy
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward, confused repetition of his formal goodbye
jokes.explanation: Small character moment showing Leslie's confusion at Ron's formal behavior during crisis.

jokes.text: Wow, don't be such a Jerry, Ben.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting healthy food options for cops
jokes.punchline: Using 'Jerry' as an insult for being overly considerate
jokes.explanation: Good use of established running gag, turning a character's name into a synonym for uncool behavior.

jokes.text: Yeah, Ben, these guys are cops, not ballerinas.
jokes.setup: Discussion about healthy food for police
jokes.punchline: Andy's simplistic gender stereotype delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy line - shows his simplistic worldview. Effective for character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Calzones are like pizzas but they're harder to eat. They're dumb and so was that idea. - Seriously? - This is embarrassing for you.
jokes.setup: Ben enthusiastically suggesting calzones
jokes.punchline: Tom's brutal, over-the-top dismissal of both calzones and Ben
jokes.explanation: Establishes Ben's calzone obsession and Tom's harsh judgment. The escalation from food criticism to personal attack is perfect.

jokes.text: So all I can think about is Captain Mustache plowing my ex-wife. - And you imagine he's wearing a cape while he's plowing her?
jokes.setup: Tom expressing jealousy about Ron and his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Ben's literal interpretation of the 'Captain' nickname
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Ben's literal superhero interpretation of Tom's metaphorical nickname creates unexpected visual.

jokes.text: You want five million orange post-its.
jokes.setup: Chris specifically says he doesn't want orange post-its
jokes.punchline: April's deliberate opposite interpretation delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect April behavior - passive-aggressive misunderstanding delivered with characteristic deadpan attitude.

jokes.text: They're getting really old and I'm an only child.
jokes.setup: Context about why Wendy is moving to Canada
jokes.punchline: The blunt, matter-of-fact way she describes family obligation
jokes.explanation: Realistic dark humor about family obligations, delivered with appropriate understatement.

jokes.text: A companion. - Hey, I don't suppose you'd want to move to Canada? - Canada. No, I don't suppose I would.
jokes.setup: Ron's formal, distant description of their relationship
jokes.punchline: His immediate, definitive rejection of moving to Canada
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Ron's American patriotism and commitment issues through formal politeness.

jokes.text: She made me this tiny sharpened stick.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining that he taught Wendy to whittle
jokes.punchline: The visual of Ron proudly showing off a basically useless whittled object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - proud of teaching traditional skills even when the result is objectively useless.

jokes.text: Look, I'm gonna tell you what I tell all my girlfriends when they get dumped. Men are dogs. - That does not apply to this situation at all.
jokes.setup: Leslie offering comfort advice to Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's logical objection to advice meant for women about men
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Leslie's caring but misapplied advice and Ron's logical response.

jokes.text: Calzones. - What the hell is wrong with this guy?
jokes.setup: Ben bringing up calzones in conversation with police chief
jokes.punchline: The chief's immediate negative reaction to Ben's calzone mention
jokes.explanation: Effective callback showing Ben hasn't learned from Tom's earlier criticism of his calzone obsession.

jokes.text: I know Tammy seems scary. But really, she's just a manipulative, psychotic, library-book-pedaling, sex-crazed she-demon.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to reassure someone about Tammy
jokes.punchline: The escalating list of increasingly extreme descriptors
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating description that builds to 'she-demon' while including specific callback to library book plot.

jokes.text: I was just tasting my new boyfriend, Glenn. - Tom.
jokes.setup: Tammy explaining intimate behavior
jokes.punchline: Tom's dejected correction of his own name while being used
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tammy's manipulation and Tom's pathetic situation, plus the innuendo.

jokes.text: Verbal altercation transpired at approximately 8:55 P.M. with a female unsub. Appears to be slightly intoxicated. Claims to be an ex-spouse. Real piece of work.
jokes.setup: Police giving official report on the Ron/Tammy incident
jokes.punchline: The overly formal police language ending with repeated 'real piece of work'
jokes.explanation: Good parody of police procedural language, with the repeated 'real piece of work' providing escalation.

jokes.text: Ron's got one just like it on his penis.
jokes.setup: Tammy showing off what appears to be a tattoo
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate revelation about matching intimate tattoos
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe humor perfectly characteristic of Tammy's boundary-crossing behavior.

jokes.text: Hi, Ann. This is April. I'm just calling to let you know that Chris can't make lunch today. I knew you wouldn't care. Good-bye.
jokes.setup: April making a professional phone call on Chris's behalf
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary cruel addition about Ann not caring
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - turning a simple message into passive-aggressive cruelty.

jokes.text: It rubbed off... From friction.
jokes.setup: Leslie noting that part of Ron's mustache is missing
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact explanation implying sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor with Ron's characteristically blunt delivery making it funnier.

jokes.text: You have been spending the last 24 hours sipping on joy juice and tripping on Tammy.
jokes.setup: Leslie staging an intervention for Ron
jokes.punchline: The drug terminology applied to relationship addiction
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting addiction metaphors to Ron's Tammy obsession.

jokes.text: I would like to address the goofy-looking, dirty-kimono-wearing, corn-rowed clown in the room.
jokes.setup: Tom addressing Ron at the intervention
jokes.punchline: The escalating series of visual descriptors for Ron's appearance
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy through descriptive language, building up the absurdity of Ron's transformed appearance.

jokes.text: I hate you like my actual brother, Levondrious, who I hate.
jokes.setup: Tom expressing his frustration with Ron
jokes.punchline: The unnecessarily specific brother comparison with redundant clarification
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Tom's characteristic over-explanation and the absurd name 'Levondrious.'

jokes.text: Ron, I do not really understand what is going on right now. But no matter what, you must keep going. No. You must stop. I love you, buddy. Follow your dreams.
jokes.setup: Andy giving advice at Ron's intervention
jokes.punchline: The completely contradictory and unhelpful advice delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - completely missing the point while being genuinely caring. The contradictions make it funnier.

jokes.text: If you're watching this, it means that once again you have danced with the devil.
jokes.setup: Ron has left himself a video warning
jokes.punchline: The dramatic metaphor for his relationship pattern
jokes.explanation: Great meta-comedy concept with Ron's dramatic language for his recurring mistake.

jokes.text: So you have two choices... One, get rid of Tammy, or two, lobotomy and castration.
jokes.setup: Past Ron giving present Ron ultimatum options
jokes.punchline: The extreme medical procedures as the alternative to breaking up
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how extreme Ron considers the Tammy situation. Dark humor with perfect character integration.

jokes.text: In fact, she's ovulating. So if you'll excuse us, we're heading off on our honeymoon.
jokes.setup: Ron announcing he and Tammy are married and leaving
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate oversharing about Tammy's fertility
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment showing how Tammy has corrupted Ron's normally private nature.

jokes.text: We bought 10 cases of Gatorade and a 40-pound bag of peanuts for energy.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining their honeymoon preparations
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous supplies clearly meant for extended sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor with clear sexual implications, delivered in Ron's matter-of-fact style.

jokes.text: Love, Bert Macklin, FBI.
jokes.setup: April reading a fake emergency letter from Ron
jokes.punchline: The letter is signed by Andy's alter-ego character
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Andy's FBI persona, showing April's clever manipulation using established character elements.

jokes.text: You mean, like calzone-sized? - Ah! Sure.
jokes.setup: Chief referencing Ben's calzone obsession when he asks for a bigger favor
jokes.punchline: The chief's immediate positive response to the calzone reference
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing how Ben's calzone reputation has followed him, but the chief likes it unlike Tom.

jokes.text: Tammy Swanson Swanson.
jokes.setup: Jerry reading the name on the new library card
jokes.punchline: The doubled surname showing they're married
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback, the doubled name creates a funny sound.

jokes.text: When I asked her to be my date, she cackled for a full minute and said, and I quote, 'anything to make Ron miserable.'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining how he got involved with Tammy
jokes.punchline: The revelation of Tammy's purely vindictive motivation
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that reframes the entire Tom subplot, showing his growing maturity.

jokes.text: Why are you hitting yourself, Glenn? Stop hitting yourself.
jokes.setup: Tammy controlling Tom during fight with Ron
jokes.punchline: The classic childhood taunt while manipulating Tom's hands
jokes.explanation: Basic slapstick humor elevated by Tammy's manipulative character and wrong name usage.

jokes.text: Oh, and by the way, last night, I faked four out of the seven. - Hmm. So did I.
jokes.setup: Tammy's parting cruel comment about their sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Ron's unexpected comeback implying he faked it too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comeback that subverts expectations about male sexuality while giving Ron a rare victory over Tammy.

jokes.text: There's a really great calzone place over in Idiotville. - Oh, really? - Down on Terrible Idea Avenue.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting they get calzones
jokes.punchline: The fake addresses mocking Ben's calzone obsession
jokes.explanation: Good callback with creative fake place names that extend the calzone mockery.

jokes.text: Calzones are pointless. They're just pizza that's harder to eat. No one likes them. Good day, sir. - Leslie, I... you know, I... - I'm just kidding. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Leslie seemingly continuing the harsh calzone criticism
jokes.punchline: The reveal that she's joking and actually wants to go get calzones
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that callbacks Tom's criticism while showing Leslie's kindness toward Ben.

jokes.text: Tom, women like scars. Shows you survived an attack, and they'll assume the attack was from a man.
jokes.setup: Tom worried about scarring from his fight
jokes.punchline: Ron's bizarre logic about scars and gender assumptions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron wisdom - confidently delivered absurd masculine logic that's both wrong and strangely convincing.
